mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth signals robust consumer demand, which drives corporate earnings and supports higher equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: The annualized rank IC of RSAFS month-over-month growth on forward 20-day returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the sign of the IC is negative in more than half of those windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above the natural rate indicates labor market slack and rising recession risk, which depresses corporate profits and equity prices over a multi-month horizon.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, the subsequent 60-day S&P 500 return is positive in more than 55% of cases, or the average return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation rises faster than the federal funds rate, real interest rates decline. Lower real rates reduce discount rates on future cash flows, boosting equity valuations and forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal (real rate decline) has a rank IC below 0.03 over a 20-day horizon in any 2-year rolling window, or the directional accuracy is below 52% in a majority of such windows.

mechanism_narrative: During the early-cycle recovery phase, accelerating payroll growth signals improving aggregate demand and earnings momentum, which positively surprises the market and pushes equity prices higher over the short term.
failure_condition: In regime periods identified as early-cycle, the 5-day forward return following a PAYEMS increase is negative more often than positive, or the average excess return over non-early periods is zero or negative.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above the 1.8 million unit threshold indicate strong construction activity and consumer confidence, which leads to positive spillover effects on employment and consumption, lifting equity markets over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1800, the subsequent 20-day S&P 500 return is negative in more than 50% of instances, or the average return is not statistically above the unconditional average over the same period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous positive month-over-month growth in industrial production and retail sales indicates broad-based economic acceleration, which drives corporate earnings growth and supports positive equity returns over a short-term horizon.
failure_condition: When both INDPRO and RSAFS rise month-over-month, the average 20-day S&P 500 return is zero or negative, or the proportion of positive returns is less than 50% (with at least 30 observations).

mechanism_narrative: GDP growth provides the most comprehensive measure of economic activity; positive real GDP growth (quarterly annualized) supports a positive equity risk premium and higher forward stock prices over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The rank IC of GDPC1 year-over-year growth on forward 60-day returns is negative over any 5-year rolling window, or the signal loses predictive power (IC < 0.02) in more than half of the years.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, successive federal funds rate increases compress equity valuations by raising the discount rate and signaling tighter financial conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: During identified tightening cycles, the average 20-day forward return is positive, or the proportion of negative returns is less than 55% over any tightening episode longer than 6 months.
